tax: Ignis Elementalis is placed at species rank beneath Ignis (Pyric order, Fire Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a roughly humanoid flame 2–3 m tall with a white-hot torso-core, distinct arm-flame extensions, and a char-ring footprint. It is separated from its sibling Ignis elementalis minor by its 2–3 m standard height and orange-yellow body versus the minor form's sub-2 m hotter-burning blue-white body; the two share the genus body-plan but never the the blue-white hotter minor form. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: sustained combustion maintained in a humanoid shape by fire-pressure along a vent-fault.
ap: Ignis Elementalis stands 2–3 m and roughly the thermal output of a forge fire. Its silhouette is a roughly humanoid column of orange-yellow flame with arm-extensions and a brighter white-hot torso-core. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a white-hot torso-core brighter than the limb-flames, visible through the body at 50 m; a char-ring footprint 50–60 cm across where it stands, charring floor stone to 2 cm depth; and arm-extensions that trail behind when it turns, leaving soot arcs on the passage walls. It moves with a floating stride that produces a char-ring at each step and scorches the air 2 m ahead, and shows orange-yellow with a white torso-core, 50 m ambient light radius in darkness. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a trail of char-rings spaced 60–70 cm apart and soot arcs on the walls from its arm-extensions, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Ignis Elementalis persists in gas-vent passages, warm fuel-rich galleries, and combustion-pressure zones of the mid-lower tiers where vent gas sustains its core. It does not breed; it forms where fire-pressure along a vent-fault ignites a combustion form into a self-sustaining humanoid shape, and recurs whenever the vent-fault refuels and the same combustion form re-ignites. It sustains its mass on vent-fault combustion pressure, drawing in ambient fuel gas drawn from the vent to sustain its core. Range is bounded by the vent-fuelled zone: outside that band the body loses the white-hot torso-core, becomes an ordinary Flameling-scale flame, and disperses. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a vent passage where gas-flames have begun taking a consistent bilateral shape against the draught, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Ignis Elementalis is roused by movement through its passage or any light source that defines the space around it and otherwise lies a still column of low flame barely distinguishable from a vent gas-flame. It claims a single vent passage or warm gallery and answers intrusion with advancing with arms extended to ignite everything within reach and corraling prey against walls. Toward grey-folk it is reactive to movement and light; survey crews report that Forgemasters approaching a vent passage in low light carry a shielded cold-lamp; an unshielded warm lamp may provoke a resting Ignis. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Ignis Elementalis: a body of sustained combustion maintained in a humanoid shape by fire-pressure along a vent-fault; immune to fire and heat; and to any physical impact, which passes through the flame body; and a defining weakness — smothering, water, and fuel-starvation, any of which collapse the torso-core within seconds. Its senses run through the medium itself: radiant-heat and fuel-gradient sensing through the vent; it tracks warm bodies by their heat signature rather than sound or sight. It tolerates complete darkness (it makes its own light), heat, and fuel-rich air but fails at smothering, flooding, and fuel-starvation. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the humanoid form, white-hot torso-core, and char-ring footprint, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Ignis Elementalis are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it advances with arms extended, igniting everything it contacts; it corrals prey against walls by filling the passage width with arm-extensions; it draws vent fuel to sustain the white-hot core under suppression attempts; and it can briefly collapse to a low flame to avoid detection, then re-expand. Each is a direct expression of fire-pressure in a vent-fault sustaining a humanoid combustion shape, so it cannot, for instance, survive smothering, flooding, or fuel-starvation; pass through stone; or strike with mass. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Ignis Elementalis matters because it is the most recognisable fire elemental in the gorge and the primary subject of Forgemaster vent-clearance protocols — its char-ring trail marks every passage where it has walked. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a vent-passage territorial hazard, logged by char-ring survey before any vent drilling begins, and its presence reshapes routes through the warm fuel-rich passages, now char-ring surveyed before drill deployment. Forgemaster tradition holds that a vent with consistent bilateral gas-flames is a forming Ignis — a signal to close the lease until the Herald has assessed the pressure behind it.
g: Ignis Elementalis (Ignis elementalis) is the standard fire elemental: a roughly humanoid body of living flame 2–3 m tall, sustained by ambient fuel gas and a self-maintaining combustion core. Documented from the gas-vent passages and warm fuel-rich galleries of the mid-lower tiers, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a roughly humanoid body of orange-yellow flame with a denser white-hot core where a torso-mass would be — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. Where the Flameling is a wandering fuel-dependent ball, the Ignis is a humanoid that holds its form and moves with intent even in lower-fuel conditions. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a char-ring on the floor in a vague humanoid outline, with soot on the walls from the arms' reach and no carcass.
